title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Browser and Node Client Systems · Contract Lens: Write Method Contracts
summary: A typed deployment console creates jobs from repeated clicks and stale tabs, requiring method semantics, preconditions, and duplicate-effect controls. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts write method contracts increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE from resource, replacement, partial-change, and repeat behavior. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use GET and HEAD with conditional, range, cache, and body expectations that preserve their defined semantics. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
